title: Jamie Brittain

infobox.value: This biographical article related to Scottish television people is a [stub](/wiki/Wikipedia:Stub "Wikipedia:Stub"). You can help Wikipedia by [adding missing information](https://en.wikipedia.org/w/index.php?title=Jamie_Brittain&action=edit).

This article about a Scottish writer, poet or playwright is a [stub](/wiki/Wikipedia:Stub "Wikipedia:Stub"). You can help Wikipedia by [adding missing information](https://en.wikipedia.org/w/index.php?title=Jamie_Brittain&action=edit).

(born 14 August 1985 in [Edinburgh](/wiki/Edinburgh "Edinburgh"), [Scotland](/wiki/Scotland "Scotland"))[[1]](#cite_note-1) is a Scottish television writer and actor. Along with his father, [Bryan Elsley](/wiki/Bryan_Elsley "Bryan Elsley"), he co-created the [E4](/wiki/E4_(TV_channel) "E4 (TV channel)") teen drama *[Skins](/wiki/Skins_(British_TV_series) "Skins (British TV series)")*.[[2]](#cite_note-2) On 1 April 2011, Brittain announced that he would be leaving the show, but he returned for the final seventh series.

*National Theatre connections 2015 : plays for young people*. Banks, Anthony,, National Theatre (Great Britain). London. 23 April 2015. [ISBN](/wiki/ISBN_(identifier) "ISBN (identifier)") [978-1-4742-3768-0](/wiki/Special:BookSources/978-1-4742-3768-0 "Special:BookSources/978-1-4742-3768-0"). ["Skins"](https://web.archive.org/web/20080719122732/http://www.timeoutsydney.com.au/timein/skins.aspx). Time Out Group. 27 February 2008. Archived from [the original](http://www.timeoutsydney.com.au/timein/skins.aspx) on 19 July 2008. Retrieved 13 June 2008.

Braithwaite, Phoebe (6 April 2018). ["Channel 4's Kiss Me First uses VR to bring its digital dystopia to life"](https://www.wired.co.uk/article/kiss-me-first-channel-4-review-e4-azanaband-tallulah-rose-haddon). *Wired UK*. [ISSN](/wiki/ISSN_(identifier) "ISSN (identifier)") [1357-0978](https://search.worldcat.org/issn/1357-0978).

From Wikipedia, the free encyclopedia

British television writer

| Jamie Brittain | |
| --- | --- |
| Born | (1985-08-14) 14 August 1985 (age 40)  [Edinburgh](/wiki/Edinburgh "Edinburgh"), Scotland |
| Occupations | Television writer and actor |
| Parent | [Bryan Elsley](/wiki/Bryan_Elsley "Bryan Elsley") |

**Jamie Brittain** (born 14 August 1985 in [Edinburgh](/wiki/Edinburgh "Edinburgh"), [Scotland](/wiki/Scotland "Scotland"))[[1]](#cite_note-1) is a Scottish television writer and actor. Along with his father, [Bryan Elsley](/wiki/Bryan_Elsley "Bryan Elsley"), he co-created the [E4](/wiki/E4_(TV_channel) "E4 (TV channel)") teen drama *[Skins](/wiki/Skins_(British_TV_series) "Skins (British TV series)")*.[[2]](#cite_note-2) On 1 April 2011, Brittain announced that he would be leaving the show, but he returned for the final seventh series.

He was an adviser and writer on the TV series *[Kiss Me First](/wiki/Kiss_Me_First_(TV_series) "Kiss Me First (TV series)")* on [Channel 4](/wiki/Channel_4 "Channel 4") television.[[3]](#cite_note-3)
